notes: THIS CONFIGURATION IS MOST OFTEN USED FOR FLEET MAINTENANCE, REPAIR AND SERVICE APPLICATIONS
THE LIFT UNIT TRANSLATES TO THE REAR APPROXIMATELY 36 1/8 DURING THE VERTICAL ARTICULATION
USE SMOOTH ELECTRICAL 90'S IN CONDUITS, NOT PLUMBING 90'S

CONDUIT SIZES & APPLICATION: A: 1 (MIN) SCHED 40 STEEL PIPE - INCOMING POWER, B: 1 (MIN) SCHED 40 STEEL PIPE - INCOMING AIRLINE, C,D: 4 SCHED 40 PVC PIPE - HYDRAULIC & AIR TO LIFT, E,F,G,H: 1 (MIN) SCHED 40 STEEL PIPE - ELECTRICAL TO LIFT

pages_summary.description: General installation notes and specifications for surface lifts with detailed requirements
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, NOTE 1, IF THE EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A DOCUMENTED MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'C= 4,000 psi AND IS AT LEAST 6 INCHES THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE USED WITHOUT ALTERATION, PROVIDED ANY FLOOR AREA WHICH IS DESIGNED FOR VEHICLES OR THE SAME WEIGHT AS THE LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY WILL BE ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT, LIFT UNIT CONTACT PRESSURES WILL BE EQUAL TO OR LESS THAN THE WHEEL CONTACT PRESSURES, SURFACE LIFTS ONLY
